q: In which year did India gain independence from British rule?
explanation: India became independent on August 15, 1947. The 'Tryst with Destiny' speech was delivered by Jawaharlal Nehru at midnight. Pakistan was created the day before on August 14.

q: Who was the first Emperor of the Mughal Empire in India?
hint: He won the First Battle of Panipat in 1526
explanation: Babur founded the Mughal Empire after defeating Ibrahim Lodi at the First Battle of Panipat (1526). He was a descendant of both Timur (Tamerlane) and Genghis Khan.

q: The French Revolution began in which year?
hint: The storming of the Bastille is its most iconic event
explanation: The French Revolution began in 1789 with the storming of the Bastille on July 14 — now celebrated as Bastille Day. It ended the monarchy and spread ideas of liberty, equality, and fraternity.

q: Which ancient wonder of the world still exists today?
hint: It's the oldest of the Seven Wonders and stands in Egypt
explanation: The Great Pyramid of Giza (~2560 BCE) is the only ancient wonder still standing. Built for Pharaoh Khufu, it was the world's tallest structure for 3,800 years!

q: Who wrote the Indian national anthem 'Jana Gana Mana'?
hint: He was also the first Asian Nobel Prize winner in Literature (1913)
explanation: Rabindranath Tagore composed Jana Gana Mana, originally a Bengali poem. It was officially adopted as the national anthem on January 24, 1950, two days before Republic Day.

q: Which war was fought between the USA and the Soviet Union primarily through proxy conflicts?
hint: It lasted from 1947 to 1991 — no direct military combat between the two superpowers
explanation: The Cold War (1947–1991) was an ideological and geopolitical conflict: USA (capitalism) vs USSR (communism). It featured the Space Race, arms race, and proxy wars in Korea, Vietnam, and Afghanistan.

q: The Magna Carta was signed in which year?
hint: It was signed by King John of England under pressure from barons
explanation: Magna Carta (Great Charter) was signed in 1215 at Runnymede, England. It was the first document to limit royal power and establish that the king must obey the law — a foundation of modern democracy.

q: Who was known as the 'Iron Man of India'?
hint: He unified 562 princely states into the Indian Union
explanation: Sardar Patel earned the title 'Iron Man of India' for his remarkable diplomatic feat of integrating 562 princely states into the Republic of India after independence — a task compared to Bismarck's unification of Germany.
